Before finalizing, score the article against this rubric:

Readability (40%): Is the writing clear and immediately understandable? Would a junior developer follow it without re-reading sentences?
Technical accuracy (35%): Are all technical claims correct? Are Git concepts described precisely?
Clarity (25%): Does the article have a clear structure and a single coherent through-line?

Scoring scale: 0-10 per criterion. Calculate the weighted composite score.
Threshold: 8.5/10.

If the composite score is below 8.5, identify the lowest-scoring criterion, revise the relevant sections to address that weakness specifically, then re-score the revised article. If the composite score is 8.5 or above, the article is complete.
